title: trifolium albopurpureum var. dichotomum
intro: Trifolium albopurpureum var. dichotomum, also known as Dichotomous White Clover, is a perennial plant in the legume family, characterized by its trifoliate leaves and white or purple flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Dichotomous White Clover are said to include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
